sections.paragraph:
m. constr. חֵיל, with suff. חֵילִי pl. חֲיָלִים (see חוּל No. 6 ), strength, power, might (especially warlike), valour, Psal. 18:33 , 40 Psa 18:40 33:16 . עָשָׂה חַיִל to shew oneself strong, to display valour, Num 24:18 Ps. 60:14 108:14 . Hence-

(2) forces, a host, Exo 14:28. שַׂר הַחַיִל leader of the army, 2Sa 24:2. בְּנֵי חַיִל, אַנְשֵׁי soldiers, Deu 3:18 1Sa 14:52 Psa 110:3, בְּיֹום חֵילֶךָ “in the day of thy warfare,” i.e. of thy warlike expedition; [that is, the day of the sending of the rod of Messiah’s strength out of Zion, when he rules in the midst of his enemies, and strikes through kings in the day of his wrath].

(3) ability, hence wealth, riches, Gen 34:29 Job 20:15. עָשָׂה חַיִל to acquire wealth; Deu 8:17, 18 Rth 4:11 Pro 31:29.

(4) virtue, uprightness, integrity, also fitness. אַנְשֵׁי חַיִל men of capacity, Gen 47:6 Exo 18:21, 25 Exo 18:25. אֵשֶׁת חַיִל a virtuous woman, Rth 3:11 Pro 12:4 31:10 . בֶּן חַיִל an honest, or upright man, 1Ki 1:52.

(5) the strength of a tree, spoken poetically of its fruits, Joe 2:22 compare כֹּחַ Job 31:39.

חיל

m. Chald.-

(1) strength, might, Dan 3:4.

(2) host, army, Dan 3:20 4:32 .

חיל

& חֵל m. properly i.q. חַיִל, especially-

(1) an army, a host, 2Ki 18:17 once חֵל Oba 1:20 also Psa 10:10, according to קרי, where חֵיל־בָּאִים may be rendered the host of the afflicted; but it is preferable to follow כתיב, see חֵילְכָה.

(2) defence, fortification, especially a particular part of the fortifications, namely, a ditch, with the antemurale surrounding it, 2Sa 20:15 Isa 26:1 Nah 3:8 Lam 2:8 comp. 1Ki 21:23 Psa 48:14 122:7 . LXX. προτείχισμα, περίτειχος. Vulg. antemurale. (In the Talmud חיל is used for a space surrounding the wall of the temple, see Lightfoot, Opp. t.ii.p. 193).
